tables_and_sources_inspected: strategy_prediction_replays (replay table: predicted_numbers, predicted_special, actual_numbers, actual_special, hit_count, special_hit, history_cutoff_draw, target_draw, generated_at, bet_index, strategy_id, lottery_type)
draws (draw-result table: draw, lottery_type, numbers, special, date, numbers_positional) - used for join-coverage check only
tests/conftest.py (canonical DB path resolution: LOTTERY_TEST_DB_PATH override, default lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db)
lottery_api/prize_aware_scorer.py (public contract / required input fields only, not imported or executed)

lottery_type_mapping.normalization: lottery_type values in strategy_prediction_replays and draws are stored as exact uppercase strings ('POWER_LOTTO', 'BIG_LOTTO', 'DAILY_539'), matching lottery_api.prize_aware_scorer.SUPPORTED_LOTTERY_TYPES exactly. No case-folding or alias mapping is required.

POWER_LOTTO.predicted_main_number_field: predicted_numbers (JSON array string, length 6, ints in [1,38], no duplicates - 36104/36104 valid)
POWER_LOTTO.actual_main_number_field: actual_numbers (JSON array string, length 6, ints in [1,38], no duplicates - 36104/36104 valid)
POWER_LOTTO.predicted_auxiliary_field: predicted_special (INTEGER 1-8, second-zone prediction). Present (non-NULL, in-range) for 9000/36104 rows (24.93%); NULL for the remaining 27104/36104 (75.07%).
POWER_LOTTO.actual_auxiliary_field: actual_special (INTEGER 1-8, second-zone result). Present for 36104/36104 rows (100%).
POWER_LOTTO.field_encoding: predicted_numbers/actual_numbers: JSON-encoded list of distinct ints; predicted_special/actual_special: plain INTEGER column
POWER_LOTTO.history_cutoff_evidence: history_cutoff_draw present for 36104/36104 rows; int(history_cutoff_draw) < int(target_draw) for 36104/36104 rows (0 violations)

BIG_LOTTO.predicted_main_number_field: predicted_numbers (JSON array string, length 6, ints in [1,49], no duplicates - 24140/24140 valid)
BIG_LOTTO.actual_main_number_field: actual_numbers (JSON array string, length 6, ints in [1,49], no duplicates - 24140/24140 valid)
BIG_LOTTO.predicted_auxiliary_field: NOT REQUIRED. predicted_special is NULL for 24140/24140 rows (100%), which is EXPECTED and CORRECT: the scorer's BIG_LOTTO special_hit_logic checks whether actual_special_number is a member of the 6 predicted_main_numbers - no separate predicted-special value is needed.
BIG_LOTTO.actual_auxiliary_field: actual_special (INTEGER 1-49, separately drawn special number). Present for 24140/24140 rows (100%); 0/24140 rows have actual_special overlapping actual_numbers (consistent with TW Lottery rules).
BIG_LOTTO.field_encoding: predicted_numbers/actual_numbers: JSON-encoded list of distinct ints; actual_special: plain INTEGER column
BIG_LOTTO.expected_cardinality: 6 main numbers (1-49) + 1 separately drawn special number (1-49, disjoint from the 6 main numbers)
BIG_LOTTO.history_cutoff_evidence: history_cutoff_draw present for 24140/24140 rows; int(history_cutoff_draw) < int(target_draw) for 24140/24140 rows (0 violations)

DAILY_539.predicted_main_number_field: predicted_numbers (JSON array string, length 5, ints in [1,39], no duplicates - 34680/34680 valid)
DAILY_539.actual_main_number_field: actual_numbers (JSON array string, length 5, ints in [1,39], no duplicates - 34680/34680 valid)
DAILY_539.predicted_auxiliary_field: NOT APPLICABLE / NOT ALLOWED. predicted_special is NULL for 34680/34680 rows (100%), consistent with the scorer's requirement that predicted_second_zone/actual_second_zone/actual_special_number all be None for DAILY_539.
DAILY_539.actual_auxiliary_field: NOT APPLICABLE / NOT ALLOWED. actual_special is NULL for 34680/34680 rows (100%), consistent with the scorer's DAILY_539 contract (no auxiliary field).
DAILY_539.field_encoding: predicted_numbers/actual_numbers: JSON-encoded list of distinct ints
DAILY_539.history_cutoff_evidence: history_cutoff_draw present for 34680/34680 rows; int(history_cutoff_draw) < int(target_draw) for 34680/34680 rows (0 violations)

POWER_LOTTO.join_cardinality: many-to-one (many replay rows per target_draw, one draws row per (lottery_type, draw) due to UNIQUE(draw, lottery_type))

POWER_LOTTO.predicted_second_zone: predicted_special (REQUIRED non-NULL by scorer; only 9000/36104 rows qualify)
POWER_LOTTO.actual_special_number: must be None (not applicable for POWER_LOTTO)

BIG_LOTTO.predicted_second_zone: must be None (not applicable for BIG_LOTTO)
BIG_LOTTO.actual_second_zone: must be None (not applicable for BIG_LOTTO)

DAILY_539.predicted_second_zone: must be None (always NULL in DB, consistent)
DAILY_539.actual_second_zone: must be None (always NULL in DB, consistent)
DAILY_539.actual_special_number: must be None (always NULL in DB, consistent)

feasibility_by_lottery.POWER_LOTTO: PARTIAL - structurally feasible only for the 9000/36104 (24.93%) rows where predicted_special (predicted second-zone) is non-NULL and in [1,8]. The remaining 27104/36104 rows (75.07%) lack a predicted second-zone value and cannot be scored via score_prize_aware_ticket without inventing a value (forbidden). All other fields (predicted/actual main numbers, actual second-zone, join, causality) are 100% available for all 36104 rows.
feasibility_by_lottery.BIG_LOTTO: FULL - 24140/24140 (100%) rows have all fields required by score_prize_aware_ticket: 6 predicted main numbers, 6 actual main numbers, and a separately-available actual special number (never overlapping the actual main numbers). No predicted-special column is required per the scorer's BIG_LOTTO special_hit_logic.
feasibility_by_lottery.DAILY_539: FULL - 34680/34680 (100%) rows have 5 predicted and 5 actual main numbers, and correctly have NO auxiliary fields populated (matching the scorer's requirement that all DAILY_539 auxiliary fields be None).

blocking_reasons_by_lottery.POWER_LOTTO: Missing predicted second-zone value (predicted_special IS NULL) for 27104/36104 rows (75.07%). This is a BLOCKING condition for FULL prize-aware replay evaluation of POWER_LOTTO, per explicit task instruction (a missing predicted second-zone must not be downgraded to a warning, must not be filled with a manufactured/random/default/most-frequent value, and must not be substituted from the actual second-zone). A future adapter MAY proceed on the 9000-row subset where predicted_special is present, excluding the other 27104 rows with an explicit exclusion reason (e.g. 'predicted_second_zone_missing').

future_adapter_design.name: prize_aware_replay_adapter (NOT IMPLEMENTED IN P271D)
future_adapter_design.description: A future, separately-authorized, read-only adapter would: (1) SELECT rows from strategy_prediction_replays for one lottery_type at a time (optionally filtered by replay_status); (2) for each row, json.loads predicted_numbers/actual_numbers and read predicted_special/actual_special; (3) for POWER_LOTTO, SKIP (exclude, with explicit reason 'predicted_second_zone_missing') any row where predicted_special IS NULL; (4) map the remaining fields to the score_prize_aware_ticket(lottery_type, predicted_main_numbers, actual_main_numbers, predicted_second_zone, actual_second_zone, actual_special_number) signature exactly as documented in scorer_input_mapping_by_lottery; (5) call lottery_api.prize_aware_scorer.score_prize_aware_ticket (call deferred to the future adapter task, NOT performed in P271D); (6) write the per-row result dict to a NEW, separate, versioned artifact (e.g. JSONL under outputs/research/), tagged with strategy_id and bet_index as pass-through identifiers only; (7) perform NO writes to strategy_prediction_replays, draws, or any other existing table; (8) leave is_m3_plus / hit_count / special_hit / existing replay columns completely untouched; (9) be independently versioned (its own scoring_version / adapter_version) and toggleable per lottery_type (e.g. POWER_LOTTO disabled-by-default or scoped to the 9000-row subset until/unless predicted second-zone coverage improves).

parallel_feature_design.principle: Any future prize-aware replay adapter is a NEW PARALLEL read-only evaluation track. It must not replace, override, migrate, deprecate, or alter existing M3+/replay scoring (hit_count, special_hit, is_m3_plus, or any existing replay column/semantics).
parallel_feature_design.no_migration_path: No schema migration, column addition, or row mutation to strategy_prediction_replays is implied or required by this audit.

next_recommended_task: HOLD / WAITING_FOR_USER_AUTHORIZATION. BIG_LOTTO and DAILY_539 replay data are structurally 100% feasible for a future read-only prize-aware adapter; POWER_LOTTO is feasible only for a 9000-row (24.93%) subset where predicted_special is populated, and is BLOCKED for full-dataset prize-aware replay evaluation until predicted second-zone coverage improves or a separate scoped POWER_LOTTO adapter is explicitly authorized. Any adapter implementation, scorer invocation, or historical evaluation requires a new explicit P271E (or later) authorization with its own STOP conditions and whitelist.
limitations: Official Taiwan Lottery prize-table pages remain MANUAL_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED (JS SPAs, not machine-readable), per P271A/P271B/P271C.

POWER_LOTTO predicted second-zone (predicted_special) is populated for only 9000/36104 (24.93%) of replay rows. A future adapter must explicitly exclude the remaining 27104 rows rather than substitute, default, or infer a value; this is a BLOCKING condition for full POWER_LOTTO prize-aware replay evaluation, not a warning.

This audit performed only structural/data-availability checks (schema, NULL counts, join coverage, causality ordering, cardinality/range/duplicate validation). It did not call lottery_api.prize_aware_scorer, did not compare predicted vs actual numbers, and did not compute hit counts, tier classes, endpoint flags, or any prize-aware/strategy-level metric.

Causality evidence is based on history_cutoff_draw < target_draw (draw-sequence ordering) and presence of a generated_at timestamp; no wall-clock prediction_cutoff_date/prediction_generated_at values were present in the inspected rows (both NULL in the sampled rows), so causality is verified via draw-sequence ordering rather than literal timestamps.
This audit is a feasibility/design document only. It does not implement, authorize, or schedule a prize-aware replay adapter, P270C, temporal-window research, or feature mining.
